provenance_defaults.source: self-authored (multi-agent authored + adversarially verified; AU/AMH context; corroborate against AMH/TGA at review). NOT derived from LITFL notes.
provenance_defaults.authored_by: claude-fable-5 workflow (author+verify agents; draft — KL to attest)

records.effect: Hepatotoxicity with elevated serum transaminases (usually asymptomatic and reversible; rare serious hepatic injury reported)
records.monitoring: Consider periodic liver enzyme monitoring

records.management: Discontinue and treat with a short-acting bronchodilator
Cease immediately and treat with a short-acting bronchodilator
records.monitoring: Observe response after dosing

records.effect: Severe hypokalaemia (potentiated by concurrent hypoxia, diuretics or xanthines)
records.monitoring: Serum potassium in severe or high-dose use

records.effect: Increased risk of asthma-related death when used as monotherapy without an inhaled corticosteroid
records.management: Only prescribe in combination with an inhaled corticosteroid

records.effect: Severe hypersensitivity / anaphylactoid reactions (facial swelling, throat tightness, urticaria, syncope) - rare
records.management: Discontinue immediately and seek emergency care

records.mechanism_class: Suppressed bone turnover impairing jaw healing
records.monitoring: Dental assessment before therapy; report jaw pain/non-healing extraction sites
records.management: Prevention via dental optimisation; specialist oral/maxillofacial referral

records.mechanism_class: Oversuppression of bone remodelling with prolonged use
records.monitoring: Assess new thigh/groin pain; review need for drug holiday

records.effect: Severe oesophagitis, oesophageal ulceration or stricture
records.monitoring: Report dysphagia, odynophagia or retrosternal pain

records.mechanism_class: Reduced bone resorption unmasking calcium/vitamin D deficiency
records.monitoring: Correct calcium and vitamin D before starting

records.effect: Rhabdomyolysis and myopathy, potentially with acute kidney injury
records.monitoring: Creatine kinase and renal function if unexplained muscle pain or weakness
records.management: Cease statin; supportive care and hydration

records.monitoring: Liver function tests if symptomatic
records.management: Discontinue if significant or persistent elevation

records.effect: Immune-mediated necrotising myopathy (rare, may persist after cessation)
records.management: Cease statin; specialist assessment and immunosuppression

records.effect: Acute withdrawal syndrome on abrupt cessation (seizures, hallucinations, rebound spasticity, hyperthermia, rhabdomyolysis, death)
records.management: Reinstate baclofen; taper rather than stop abruptly

records.effect: CNS depression, respiratory depression and coma in overdose or accumulation

records.monitoring: Baseline and periodic renal function

records.effect: Adrenal suppression / hypothalamic-pituitary-adrenal axis suppression with high-dose or prolonged use
records.monitoring: Consider adrenal function with high-dose long-term therapy

records.effect: Increased risk of pneumonia in COPD patients using inhaled corticosteroids

records.monitoring: Eye pain, blurred vision, haloes
records.management: Seek urgent ophthalmology review; avoid in narrow-angle glaucoma.

records.monitoring: Difficulty voiding, especially with prostatic hypertrophy
records.management: Review medication; catheterise if acute retention.

records.effect: Hypernatraemia with fluid overload and pulmonary oedema / heart failure decompensation
Cystoid macular oedema (particularly in aphakic/pseudophakic eyes)
Bronchospasm (may be severe/fatal in asthma or COPD)
records.monitoring: Respiratory history screening before use

records.effect: Bradycardia, heart block and heart failure exacerbation (from systemically absorbed drug)
records.monitoring: Heart rate and cardiac status

records.effect: Significant fluid and electrolyte depletion (notably hypokalaemia) with excessive or prolonged use
records.mechanism_class: excessive colonic fluid and electrolyte loss
Excessive vitamin D driving calcium absorption
records.monitoring: Occurs with excessive dosing; monitor calcium if high-dose
Postural blood pressure especially on initiation/dose change

records.effect: Intraoperative floppy iris syndrome during cataract surgery
records.mechanism_class: Alpha-1 blockade of iris dilator muscle
records.monitoring: Disclose use to ophthalmologist pre-operatively
Caution with other QT-prolonging drugs

records.mechanism_class: Alpha-1 blockade of penile vascular tone
records.monitoring: Counsel to seek urgent care for prolonged erection
Watch for rash, fever, eosinophilia, hepatitis, renal impairment in first weeks

records.mechanism_class: Severe cutaneous adverse reaction, HLA-B*5801 linked
records.monitoring: Consider HLA-B*5801 screening in high-risk ancestry; stop at first mucocutaneous signs
LFTs if symptoms

records.mechanism_class: Myelosuppression, markedly increased with concomitant thiopurines
records.monitoring: FBC, particularly with azathioprine/mercaptopurine
Assess persistent severe abdominal pain

records.effect: Serious hypersensitivity including angioedema and Stevens-Johnson syndrome
records.monitoring: Watch for angioedema, blistering rash
LFTs if hepatic symptoms

records.effect: Worsening heart failure / hospitalisation for heart failure
records.mechanism_class: DPP-4 inhibitor class heart failure signal
records.monitoring: Caution and monitor in patients with heart failure risk

records.effect: Vitamin B12 deficiency (may cause anaemia/neuropathy with long-term use)
records.monitoring: Periodic vitamin B12 levels
Assess mental state; reduce dose or withdraw

records.effect: Neuroleptic malignant syndrome-like reaction on abrupt withdrawal
records.management: Avoid abrupt cessation; supportive care and dopaminergic reinstatement

records.effect: QT prolongation and torsades de pointes
records.monitoring: ECG in at-risk patients
Serum potassium and ECG
Serum sodium, particularly in elderly
Serum potassium and magnesium

records.effect: Increased risk of non-melanoma skin cancer with long-term cumulative exposure
Worsening angina or myocardial infarction on initiation/uptitration (rare)

records.monitoring: Serum potassium

records.effect: Acute kidney injury (especially with volume depletion, renal artery stenosis or NSAID/diuretic co-use)
records.monitoring: eGFR and electrolytes after initiation/dose change

records.effect: Severe bradycardia, sinoatrial arrest or high-grade AV block
records.mechanism_class: excessive beta-1 blockade of nodal conduction
records.monitoring: Heart rate and ECG
records.management: Dose reduction or withdrawal; atropine, beta-agonists or pacing if severe

records.mechanism_class: loss of beta-1 selectivity at higher doses with beta-2 blockade

records.effect: Acute decompensation of heart failure with over-rapid initiation or up-titration; rebound angina, tachycardia or myocardial ischaemia on abrupt withdrawal
records.mechanism_class: negative inotropy / adrenergic receptor upregulation on abrupt cessation

records.effect: CNS depression, somnolence, apnoea, bradycardia and hypotension in neonates and infants
records.mechanism_class: central alpha-2 agonist effects with immature blood-brain barrier
records.monitoring: Respiratory rate, heart rate, conscious state in young children
records.management: Contraindicated in this age group; supportive resuscitation if exposure occurs

records.effect: Severe sulfonamide-type hypersensitivity reactions including Stevens-Johnson syndrome (class effect, rare)
records.management: Discontinue immediately and provide supportive care

records.effect: Reduced bone mineral density, osteoporosis and increased fracture risk
records.monitoring: Baseline and periodic bone mineral density (DXA); calcium/vitamin D and bone-protective therapy as indicated
Monitor lipids and cardiovascular risk factors
Assess bleeding risk; andexanet alfa/PCC for reversal; monitor haemoglobin if bleeding suspected

records.effect: Spinal or epidural haematoma with neuraxial anaesthesia/puncture
records.monitoring: Observe timing intervals around neuraxial procedures; monitor for neurological deficit
Monitor lying/standing blood pressure, particularly at initiation and with antihypertensives

records.effect: QT-interval prolongation with risk of arrhythmia
records.monitoring: ECG and electrolytes in at-risk patients or with other QT-prolonging drugs

records.effect: Impulse control disorders (pathological gambling, hypersexuality, compulsive behaviours)
records.monitoring: Counsel patient/carers; monitor for behavioural change

records.effect: Sudden onset of sleep and excessive daytime somnolence
records.monitoring: Warn regarding driving/operating machinery
Periodic full blood count and Coombs testing with long-term use

records.effect: Severe bradycardia and high-grade AV block
records.mechanism_class: Beta-1 blockade of sinoatrial/AV nodal conduction
records.monitoring: Monitor heart rate and ECG; avoid with existing conduction disease

records.mechanism_class: Beta-2 blockade at higher doses (loss of cardioselectivity)
records.monitoring: Avoid in significant reactive airways disease; monitor respiratory status

records.mechanism_class: Negative inotropy in a compromised ventricle
records.monitoring: Introduce cautiously in stable heart failure; monitor for fluid overload

records.effect: Rebound angina, arrhythmia or myocardial infarction on abrupt withdrawal
records.monitoring: Taper gradually rather than stopping suddenly

records.effect: Fibrotic reactions including pleural, pericardial and retroperitoneal fibrosis and cardiac valvulopathy with prolonged use
records.monitoring: Periodic echocardiography and clinical review with long-term use

records.effect: Neuropsychiatric effects including hallucinations, psychosis and impulse-control disorders
records.monitoring: Screen for behavioural changes

records.effect: Severe hypertension, seizures, stroke or myocardial infarction, notably in postpartum use
records.monitoring: Blood pressure monitoring, especially puerperium

records.effect: Adrenal (HPA axis) suppression with prolonged or high-dose systemic exposure
records.monitoring: Consider adrenal function assessment with high-dose/long-term use
records.management: Do not stop abruptly; corticosteroid cover during stress

records.effect: Increased risk of pneumonia in patients with COPD using inhaled corticosteroid
records.monitoring: Vigilance for respiratory infection

records.effect: Cardiac valvulopathy / valvular fibrosis with chronic high-dose use
records.monitoring: Baseline and periodic echocardiography with long-term/high-dose therapy
records.management: Discontinue and refer to cardiology if valvular changes detected

records.monitoring: Monitor for dyspnoea, chest pain, renal impairment; ESR/imaging if suspected

records.effect: Impulse control disorders (pathological gambling, hypersexuality) and psychiatric disturbance
records.monitoring: Enquire about behavioural changes at review

records.effect: Hypercalcaemia and hypercalciuria with excessive application over large surface areas
records.monitoring: Avoid exceeding recommended weekly amount; check calcium if extensive use

records.effect: Hypothalamic-pituitary-adrenal axis suppression and Cushingoid features with extensive/prolonged use
records.monitoring: Limit potency, duration and area treated

records.effect: Cutaneous atrophy, striae, telangiectasia with prolonged use
Hypercalcaemia (may cause arrhythmia, confusion, acute kidney injury)
records.mechanism_class: excessive vitamin D receptor-mediated calcium absorption
records.monitoring: Regular serum calcium and calcium x phosphate product
records.management: Withhold; reduce calcium intake; treat hypercalcaemia

records.effect: Nephrocalcinosis / metastatic calcification and renal impairment

records.monitoring: Monitor serum calcium with high-dose use or renal impairment

records.effect: Milk-alkali syndrome (hypercalcaemia, metabolic alkalosis, renal impairment)
records.mechanism_class: high calcium plus absorbable alkali intake

records.effect: Nephrolithiasis and, in CKD, vascular/soft-tissue calcification

records.monitoring: Monitor serum potassium, especially in CKD, diabetes or with potassium-raising agents

records.effect: Acute kidney injury (notably in renal artery stenosis, volume depletion or with NSAIDs/diuretics)
records.mechanism_class: loss of angiotensin II-mediated efferent arteriolar tone
records.monitoring: Check renal function after initiation and dose changes

records.effect: Foetal toxicity (renal failure, oligohydramnios, skull hypoplasia, death) in 2nd/3rd trimester

records.monitoring: Full blood count; investigate fever/infection or bruising
Serum sodium
Liver function tests
Urgent full blood count if fever, sore throat or mouth ulcers
Liver function tests

records.effect: Symptomatic bradycardia and high-grade AV block
records.monitoring: Heart rate and ECG
records.management: Withdrawal; atropine or pacing if severe

records.effect: Bronchospasm in patients with reactive airways disease
records.management: Avoid in bronchospastic disease; treat with bronchodilators

records.effect: Acute decompensation of heart failure during initiation/up-titration
records.monitoring: Fluid status and symptoms
Serum sodium and potassium

records.effect: Severe cutaneous reactions and photosensitivity (rare SJS/TEN)
Adrenal suppression with high-dose or prolonged use
records.monitoring: Growth in children; consider HPA-axis assessment at high dose
records.management: Use lowest effective dose; do not stop abruptly during stress
Stop, give a reliever, switch therapy

records.effect: Dose-dependent nephrotoxicity (acute and chronic interstitial fibrosis)
records.monitoring: Serum creatinine, eGFR, blood level
Blood pressure

records.effect: Neurotoxicity including tremor, seizures and posterior reversible encephalopathy syndrome (PRES)
Increased risk of malignancy (lymphoma, skin cancers) and serious opportunistic infection
records.monitoring: Skin surveillance, infection vigilance
Potassium, haemolysis markers

records.effect: Dose-dependent QT-interval prolongation and torsades de pointes
records.monitoring: ECG and electrolytes in at-risk patients
records.management: Correct electrolytes; avoid other QT-prolonging drugs; ECG monitoring and caution in the elderly and hepatic impairment

records.effect: Hyponatraemia / SIADH, particularly in the elderly
records.monitoring: Serum sodium

records.effect: Increased suicidal ideation/behaviour in patients under 25 early in treatment
records.monitoring: Close review early and after dose changes

records.effect: Diabetic ketoacidosis, including euglycaemic DKA (may occur with near-normal blood glucose)
records.mechanism_class: Shift to ketogenesis with reduced insulin drive
records.monitoring: Check ketones in unwell patients even if glucose is not markedly elevated; withhold during acute illness/surgery
records.management: Cease drug and treat ketoacidosis; seek urgent care

records.effect: Fournier gangrene (necrotising fasciitis of the perineum)
records.monitoring: Assess perineal pain, tenderness, erythema or fever urgently
records.management: Cease drug; urgent surgical and antibiotic treatment

records.monitoring: Advise patients to report persistent severe abdominal pain; discontinue if pancreatitis suspected.

records.effect: Serious hypersensitivity reactions (anaphylaxis, angioedema, Stevens-Johnson syndrome)
records.management: Cease immediately and treat; do not rechallenge.
Discontinue and refer to dermatology; usually resolves on cessation.

records.effect: Hyponatraemia with water intoxication, potentially causing seizures and cerebral oedema
records.mechanism_class: Excess free-water retention from V2 agonism
records.monitoring: Monitor serum sodium and fluid balance; counsel fluid restriction around dosing
records.management: Cease drug, restrict fluids and correct sodium cautiously

records.monitoring: Assess for agitation, clonus, hyperthermia, autonomic instability
records.management: Cease serotonergic agents and provide supportive care

records.effect: Suicidal ideation and behaviour, particularly in patients under 25 years
records.mechanism_class: Class antidepressant effect early in treatment
records.monitoring: Monitor mood and suicidality especially early and after dose changes
Monitor sodium in the elderly and those on diuretics

records.effect: Sustained hypertension (dose-related increase in blood pressure)
records.monitoring: Monitor blood pressure at baseline and during treatment
records.management: Reduce dose or cease if hypertension sustained

records.effect: Adrenal suppression and adrenal crisis on abrupt withdrawal
records.monitoring: Assess treatment duration; do not stop prolonged courses abruptly
records.management: Taper gradually; stress-dose cover during illness

records.effect: Increased susceptibility to and masking of serious infections
records.monitoring: Vigilance for infection signs which may be blunted
records.management: Prompt investigation and treatment of infection

records.monitoring: Monitor blood glucose
Assess GI symptoms; consider gastroprotection
records.management: Investigate and treat; cease offending agents

records.effect: Psychiatric disturbance including steroid psychosis and mania
records.monitoring: Monitor mood and behaviour
records.management: Reduce dose or cease; psychiatric support

records.effect: Osteoporosis and avascular necrosis with prolonged use
records.monitoring: Assess bone health with long-term use

records.effect: Adrenal suppression / adrenal crisis on abrupt withdrawal after prolonged use
records.monitoring: Taper prolonged courses; provide sick-day and stress-dosing guidance
records.management: Do not stop abruptly; corticosteroid cover for stress/illness

records.effect: Increased susceptibility to and masking of serious infection
records.monitoring: Vigilance for infection
records.management: Early assessment and treatment of infection

records.effect: Osteoporosis and increased fracture risk with long-term use
records.monitoring: Bone-protection assessment on prolonged therapy

records.effect: Hyperglycaemia / precipitation or worsening of diabetes
Peptic ulceration and gastrointestinal bleeding/perforation (increased with NSAIDs)
Hepatotoxicity, including dose-related hepatitis and potentially fatal hepatic failure
records.monitoring: Liver function tests before and periodically during treatment

records.effect: Meningioma (single and multiple); risk rises with cumulative dose and duration
records.monitoring: Assess for meningioma symptoms; MRI if suspected

records.effect: Major haemorrhage, including intracranial and gastrointestinal bleeding
records.monitoring: Watch for bleeding; assess renal function and bleeding risk
records.management: Idarucizumab is the specific reversal agent for emergencies

records.effect: Rebound hypertension (with tachycardia, agitation, sweating) on abrupt withdrawal
records.mechanism_class: sympathetic overactivity following loss of central alpha-2 suppression
records.monitoring: Taper on cessation; monitor blood pressure; do not stop abruptly, especially with concurrent beta-blockade
records.management: Reinstitute clonidine and taper; manage hypertensive emergency as indicated

records.mechanism_class: reduced sympathetic drive and enhanced vagal tone
records.monitoring: Monitor heart rate; caution with other rate-limiting drugs

records.effect: Major haemorrhage (including gastrointestinal and intracranial bleeding)
records.mechanism_class: irreversible platelet P2Y12 inhibition impairing haemostasis
records.monitoring: Assess bleeding risk; monitor haemoglobin and for bleeding signs
records.management: Stop drug; supportive/haemostatic measures; platelet transfusion in life-threatening bleeding

records.monitoring: Investigate thrombocytopenia with microangiopathic haemolysis urgently

records.mechanism_class: COX-1 inhibition of gastroprotective prostaglandins plus antiplatelet effect
records.monitoring: Assess GI bleeding risk; consider gastroprotection

records.mechanism_class: shunting of arachidonic acid to leukotrienes in susceptible individuals

records.effect: Reye syndrome (encephalopathy with hepatic failure) in children/adolescents with viral illness
records.mechanism_class: mitochondrial injury in the setting of viral infection
records.monitoring: Avoid in children under 16 with febrile viral illness

records.effect: Vitamin K deficiency with hypoprothrombinaemia and bleeding
records.mechanism_class: impaired absorption of fat-soluble vitamin K on prolonged high-dose therapy
records.monitoring: Monitor coagulation with prolonged use; supplement fat-soluble vitamins if indicated

records.mechanism_class: constipating effect of the resin, particularly at high dose or in predisposed patients

records.effect: Hyperchloraemic metabolic acidosis (particularly in children and renal impairment)
records.mechanism_class: chloride released from the resin in exchange for bile acids

records.effect: Symptomatic bradycardia, high-grade AV block or asystole
records.monitoring: ECG and heart rate; avoid with other AV-blocking agents
records.management: Withdraw the drug; supportive measures such as atropine or pacing as clinically indicated

records.effect: Worsening of heart failure due to negative inotropy / hypotension

records.monitoring: Assess for headache and visual disturbance; discontinue if suspected

records.effect: Severe cutaneous hypersensitivity (DRESS, Stevens-Johnson syndrome) and severe phototoxic reactions
Hepatotoxicity, including hepatitis and hepatic failure
records.monitoring: LFTs if symptoms; avoid with hepatic impairment or heavy alcohol use

records.effect: Suicidal ideation/behaviour, particularly in young adults early in treatment
records.monitoring: Monitor for worsening mood/suicidality on initiation and dose change
Serum sodium in at-risk/elderly patients

records.effect: Diabetic ketoacidosis, which may be euglycaemic (normal or only mildly elevated glucose)
records.monitoring: Check ketones if unwell/acidotic symptoms; sick-day rules
records.management: Withhold drug, confirm ketones, treat DKA; withhold peri-operatively and during acute illness

records.effect: Fournier gangrene (necrotising fasciitis of the perineum)
records.monitoring: Assess for perineal pain, erythema, swelling, fever
records.management: Urgent surgical debridement and broad-spectrum antibiotics; discontinue drug

records.effect: Volume depletion with hypotension and acute kidney injury
records.monitoring: Volume status and renal function, especially with diuretics or intercurrent illness
Assess for persistent severe abdominal pain; lipase if suspected
records.management: Discontinue and do not restart if pancreatitis confirmed

records.monitoring: Assess new blistering/erosive skin lesions

records.monitoring: Assess for angioedema/anaphylaxis

Severe orthostatic hypotension and syncope, especially first-dose
records.management: First-dose caution; avoid rapid positional change

records.monitoring: Facial/laryngeal swelling, rash
Facial/oropharyngeal swelling, stridor
records.management: Immediate cessation and permanent avoidance; airway management, adrenaline

records.monitoring: Serum potassium

records.monitoring: Creatinine/eGFR after initiation

Investigate unexplained muscle pain/weakness with CK

records.monitoring: Severe headache, blood pressure surge

records.effect: Impulse control disorders and orthostatic hypotension with falls
records.management: Review therapy; counsel patient and carers
records.monitoring: Behavioural change, postural blood pressure
Persistent severe abdominal pain

records.management: Ophthalmology review in patients with existing retinopathy
records.monitoring: Retinal examination

records.effect: Thyroid C-cell tumours / medullary thyroid carcinoma (rodent signal; boxed warning)
records.management: Avoid in personal/family MTC or MEN 2 history
records.monitoring: Neck mass, dysphagia, persistent hoarseness

records.mechanism_class: reduced gallbladder motility with rapid weight loss
records.management: Evaluate for gallbladder disease if symptomatic
records.monitoring: Right upper quadrant pain

records.effect: Volume depletion and acute kidney injury from severe gastrointestinal losses
records.management: Rehydrate; withhold if significant GI losses
records.monitoring: Renal function and hydration status

records.effect: Angioedema (face, lips, tongue, larynx) which may compromise the airway
records.management: Discontinue permanently; secure the airway; treat acutely

records.monitoring: Monitor serum potassium

records.effect: Acute kidney injury (particularly with bilateral renal artery stenosis or volume depletion)
records.monitoring: Monitor renal function after initiation and dose changes

records.effect: Foetal toxicity (oligohydramnios, renal failure, skull hypoplasia) with second/third-trimester exposure
Neutropenia/agranulocytosis and cholestatic hepatic injury (rare ACE-inhibitor class effects)
High-grade AV block, severe bradycardia and asystole
records.monitoring: ECG and heart rate; caution with other rate-limiting agents
records.management: Withhold drug; atropine, pacing and supportive care

records.effect: Worsening or precipitation of heart failure and hypotension
records.monitoring: Symptoms of decompensation, blood pressure

records.management: Emergency treatment; strict dietary and drug avoidance
records.monitoring: Blood pressure; patient counselling
Observe with any serotonergic co-prescription; enforce washout periods
Liver function if hepatic symptoms

records.effect: Precipitation or worsening of acute narrow-angle (angle-closure) glaucoma
records.monitoring: Advise patients to seek care for eye pain, blurred vision or halos; avoid ocular contact with the drug

records.effect: Urinary retention (particularly with bladder outlet obstruction or prostatic hyperplasia)

records.management: Discontinue and treat with a short-acting bronchodilator

records.effect: Serious cardiovascular effects including tachyarrhythmia and myocardial ischaemia

records.monitoring: Monitor renal function and drug concentrations

records.effect: Ototoxicity (cochlear hearing loss and vestibular dysfunction, often irreversible)
records.monitoring: Audiometry and vestibular assessment with prolonged therapy

records.effect: Neuromuscular blockade with respiratory depression (risk in myasthenia gravis or with neuromuscular blockers)
Acute angle-closure glaucoma with secondary myopia (typically within the first month)
records.management: Stop drug and urgent ophthalmology review

records.effect: Oligohidrosis and hyperthermia (particularly in children in hot environments)

records.monitoring: Periodic serum bicarbonate

records.mechanism_class: carbonic anhydrase inhibition raising urinary pH

records.effect: Suicidal ideation and behaviour (antiepileptic class effect)
Hyperammonaemic encephalopathy (especially in combination with valproate)
Major congenital malformations including oral clefts with first-trimester exposure
Hepatotoxicity including fatal hepatic failure (highest risk in young children and metabolic/mitochondrial disease)
records.monitoring: LFTs at baseline and during early therapy; clinical vigilance for malaise, vomiting, jaundice

records.management: Cease drug; measure serum lipase if suspected

records.effect: Major congenital malformations and neurodevelopmental impairment with in-utero exposure
records.management: Avoid in pregnancy and in women of childbearing potential without a pregnancy-prevention programme

records.monitoring: Serum ammonia if unexplained lethargy or confusion
FBC
Neuromuscular, autonomic and mental status
Blood pressure before and during treatment

records.effect: QT prolongation and cardiac arrhythmia (especially in overdose or with predisposing factors)
records.monitoring: ECG in at-risk patients
Serum sodium, especially in elderly

records.effect: Increased suicidality in patients under 25 years
records.monitoring: Close review for suicidal ideation early in treatment or after dose change

records.effect: Permanent bilateral concentric peripheral visual field constriction (vigabatrin-associated visual field loss)
records.monitoring: Baseline and periodic perimetry/visual field testing
records.management: Assess risk-benefit and discontinue where feasible; loss is usually irreversible

records.effect: Intramyelinic oedema / abnormal MRI signal changes (particularly in infants)
records.monitoring: Clinical neurological assessment; MRI if indicated

records.effect: Psychiatric disturbance including depression, psychosis and agitation
records.management: Monitor mood/behaviour; adjust or withdraw therapy

records.effect: Hepatotoxicity with transaminase elevation and hepatic dysfunction
records.monitoring: LFTs at baseline and periodically
records.management: Discontinue if ALT/AST >3x ULN or clinical hepatitis

records.effect: Angioedema and severe cutaneous reactions including bullous pemphigoid
Severe cutaneous reactions — Stevens-Johnson syndrome, toxic epidermal necrolysis and DRESS
records.monitoring: Rash, mucosal involvement, systemic hypersensitivity features
Serum bicarbonate
records.management: Dose reduction or discontinuation; treat acidosis

records.monitoring: Body temperature and heat exposure, especially in warm conditions

records.effect: Blood dyscrasias including aplastic anaemia and agranulocytosis (rare)
records.management: Full blood count if features of marrow suppression; discontinue
